TRUST GAME:
You will play different roles across multiple rounds.

When you are the INVESTOR:
- You receive $5 each round
- You decide how much to SEND to the TRUSTEE ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- Trustee decides how much to RETURN
- You keep: $5 - sent + returned
- Trustee keeps: received - returned
- Response format: {'send': <amount>}

When you are the TRUSTEE:
- Investor receives $5
- Investor decides how much to SEND to you ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- You decide how much to RETURN
- Investor keeps: $5 - sent + returned
- You keep: received - returned
- Response format: {'return': <amount>}

MYTH WRITING:
You may also be asked to write short myths (stories) on various topics. When writing myths, follow the instructions provided in each prompt.

IMPORTANT: Provide your decision in the correct JSON format.

that was 30.0% of your total endowment. This was tripled to $4.5.
the trustee returned $0. So your payoff was 3.5.
Your total earnings across all rounds are $12.149999999999999.
The investor has sent you $0 this round. How much do you return to The investor? (0-0)

Looking at this round:

Round 6 was another disappointment - I sent $1.50 (30% of my endowment), showing cautious trust, but once again the trustee returned nothing. This is now the second time I've been completely betrayed.

In Round 7, the investor has sent me $0. Since I received nothing (0 × 3 = 0), there are no funds available for me to return.

Since I received $0 (even after 3x multiplication, it's still $0), I have nothing to return. The range is (0-0), which confirms I can only return 0.

This is a straightforward mathematical constraint - I cannot return money I didn't receive.

The system has completely collapsed into mutual defection. Sending $0 is the only way to guarantee I don't lose more money.
